title: Qual Loop
problem: Kyten's whole pitch is a three week cycle: 1 week to design -> 1 week to qualify -> 1 week to volume. The 1 week to qualify claim is the most aggressive - real world aerospace battery qualification under DO 160G + UN38.3 + customer specific abuse profiles takes 8 - 16 weeks at most suppliers because test campaigns are sequential, instrumentation is hand stitched per build, and pass/fail decisions wait on a human engineer to read scope traces.
direction: A test orchestration runtime + digital twin for aerospace battery qualification: schedules DO 160G + UN38.3 campaigns in maximum parallel, predicts pass/fail from the first 10% of each test, and emits a regulator grade evidence bundle.
why: Direction asymmetric early stop is non obvious. It is also the only technically honest way to defend "1 week to qualify." Per datapoint content addressed provenance is the artifact a defense aerospace prime's supplier quality engineer will care about more than any feature on the homepage.

archetypes.name: kyten evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: kyten signal changes while whole context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: whole boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: whole handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: pitch regression harness
archetypes.trigger: pitch behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: three operator packet
archetypes.trigger: three output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
